Pranav
Kumar.

I build deep-learning and computer-vision systems for the messy conditions of the real world — where models meet noise, distribution shift, and adversaries. My work sits at the seam between research and shipping: reproducing recent literature, then turning it into reliable, reproducible code.

01 Two modes, one engineer researcher / builder

I'm drawn to problems where a model that looks perfect on a benchmark falls apart the moment reality perturbs it.

As a researcher, I read and reproduce recent literature, design controlled experiments, and translate published methods into working code — currently studying adversarial robustness at the Trustworthy BiometraVision Lab.

As an engineer, I ship end-to-end ML pipelines: preprocessing, segmentation, training, evaluation, and the unglamorous plumbing in between. I care about clean, reproducible code and systems that hold up outside the lab.

The throughline is trust — building vision systems that stay reliable and robust when the inputs get noisy, shifted, or actively hostile.

Research interests

Adversarial robustness Image segmentation Model reliability Diffusion Models Agentic AI VLMs LLMs NLP

03 Selected project computer vision

Plantation Monitoring System

Drone CV · at scale

An end-to-end pipeline that detects unwanted plant growth from drone images, built toward online farm monitoring at scale. From raw aerial frames to a per-region classification decision — every stage designed to stay robust on real, uneven field imagery.
